topic: CRTouch Z-offset and first-layer calibration (M851, babystepping, BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET)
summary:
The Hoverfly runs a CRTouch in BLTOUCH mode with NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET { -30, -40, -3.49 }, where -3.49 is the current nominal nozzle-to-probe Z distance and the live first-layer "squish" control; it almost always needs hand fine-tuning since the firmware default only reflects mounting geometry. The biggest workflow consequence of the current config is that BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET is DISABLED. With it disabled, BABYSTEPPING + DOUBLECLICK_FOR_Z_BABYSTEPPING still let you double-click the status screen during a print to nudge Z live (M290 internally), but those babysteps are a temporary, session-only Z shift. They are NOT folded into the saved probe Z offset and are lost on the next G28/reset; M500 will not capture them. To make a tuned value permanent you must read the total babystep, then bake it into M851 Z (new Z = old Z + babystep) and run M500 (or edit NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET and reflash). This is the standard, deterministic Marlin workflow and a reasonable choice, but the user must understand the two-step 'tune live, then transcribe to M851 + M500' pattern or their good first layer silently reverts. Enabling BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET would instead make babysteps adjust the probe Z offset directly so one M500 saves them, at the cost of some known edge-case bugs (Z stepper disable mid-print on older builds, UBL revert issues, neither applies here since this build uses BILINEAR and current Marlin). Sign convention: a MORE NEGATIVE M851 Z / NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET Z moves the nozzle CLOSER to the bed (more squish); less negative lifts it. For a CRTouch the pin sits ~2-3 mm below the retracted nozzle, so the offset is always negative and typically lands around -2 to -4 mm. Good first layer (Ellis): lines fused with no gaps, no light visible between them, surface flat and slightly matte, only tiny pinholes where perimeters meet infill. Too little squish (not negative enough): gaps between lines, see-through to bed, poor adhesion, separated rounded strands. Too much squish (too negative): lines smear into a glassy wavy surface, ripples/washboarding, excess hairs/ridges, elephant-foot, possible clog from back-pressure. Ellis recommends erring slightly toward MORE squish. Test with a single-layer patch / Ellis scattered-square pattern using first-layer height >=0.25 mm and first-layer line width >=120% for clearer feedback. CRTouch note: current Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_SLOW 120 mm/min is conservative and reliable; if probing is erratic, community fix is raising it toward ~960 mm/min or checking HS mode.</summary>
<procedure>FIRST-LAYER / Z-OFFSET CALIBRATION (current config: BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET disabled)

PREP
1. Mechanically level/tram the bed, run G28, then G29 to build the 4x4 bilinear mesh. Heat bed + nozzle to printing temps first (PREHEAT_BEFORE_LEVELING is on). Clean the build surface.
2. In the slicer set FIRST LAYER HEIGHT >= 0.25 mm and FIRST-LAYER LINE WIDTH >= 120% (e.g. 0.48 mm on a 0.4 nozzle) to exaggerate good/bad feedback (Ellis).

ROUGH SET
3. Confirm current offset: send M851 (reports X-30 Y-40 Z-3.49). This Z is your starting squish.

LIVE TUNE (the actual test)
4. Start a first-layer test: Ellis' scattered-square pattern, or a single-layer large thin square / bed-level-test STL.
5. While the FIRST layer prints, DOUBLE-CLICK the status screen (DOUBLECLICK_FOR_Z_BABYSTEPPING) to open live Z babystepping. Watch the line being laid down.
   - More negative / down = more squish (closer to bed).
   - Less negative / up = less squish.
   - Adjust in 0.01-0.02 mm steps; let a few lines print after each change before judging.
6. WRITE DOWN the total babystep dialed in (e.g. -0.12 mm). With BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET disabled this value is temporary and lost on the next home/reset.

MAKE IT PERMANENT (required with current config)
7. New Z offset = old M851 Z + babystep. Example: -3.49 + (-0.12) = -3.61.
8. Send M851 Z-3.61  then  M500 to save to EEPROM (or edit NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET Z and reflash).
9. Re-home (G28), re-run G29 if desired, and reprint the test to confirm.

PASS / FAIL CRITERIA
GOOD (PASS): adjacent lines fused, NO gaps and NO light between them; flat uniformly matte/slightly textured surface; only tiny pinholes where perimeter meets infill; square peels off as one solid even-thickness sheet.
TOO HIGH / UNDER-SQUISHED (FAIL): gaps between lines, bed/light visible through it, rounded separated lines, weak adhesion or lifting corners. Fix: make Z offset MORE negative.
TOO LOW / OVER-SQUISHED (FAIL): smeared glossy/wavy surface, ripples/washboarding, transparent thin spots, hairs/ridges, elephant-foot, under-extrusion from back-pressure. Fix: make Z offset LESS negative.
When unsure, lean slightly toward MORE squish (Ellis) for adhesion reliability.

ALTERNATIVE (paper method, rough only): G28, move Z to 0, slide paper under nozzle, lower until slight drag; use that Z as a starting point, always verify with a printed first layer.</procedure>
<stlModel>Ellis' first-layer-squish scattered-square pattern (or any single-layer bed-level-test / large thin square ~180x180 mm)</stlModel>
<recommendations>[{"title":"Understand the two-step workflow forced by BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET being disabled","priority":"High","currentValue":"BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET disabled; BABYSTEPPING + DOUBLECLICK_FOR_Z_BABYSTEPPING enabled","recommendedValue":"Keep disabled, but adopt: tune live with double-click babystep, then transcribe the total into M851 Z and run M500","rationale":"With BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET disabled, double-click babysteps during a print are a temporary session-only Z shift; they are NOT written into the saved probe Z offset and are discarded on the next G28/restart, and M500 will not capture them. A perfectly dialed-in first layer silently reverts unless you read off the babystep amount and bake it into M851 Z (new Z = old Z + babystep) followed by M500. This is a valid deterministic setup, but only if the user follows the transcribe step; it is the single most important thing to know about this machine's first-layer workflow.","howToApply":"After live-tuning, note total babystep (e.g. -0.12). Send M851 Z<old + babystep> (e.g. M851 Z-3.61), then M500. Or edit Configuration.h NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET { -30, -40, -3.61 } and reflash.","sources":["https://marlinfw.org/docs/gcode/M290.html","https://github.com/MarlinFirmware/Marlin/issues/19899","https://marlinfw.org/docs/gcode/M851.html"]},{"title":"Optionally enable BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET for one-step live save","priority":"Medium","currentValue":"// #define BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET (disabled)","recommendedValue":"#define BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET (enable if you prefer babystep -> M500 in one step)","rationale":"Enabling it makes live babysteps adjust the probe Z offset directly, so a single M500 saves your tuned first layer and it persists across restarts, a friendlier workflow for frequent surface/filament changes. The historically cited bugs (Z steppers disabling mid-print, offset reverting) are tied to UBL or older Marlin builds; this machine uses AUTO_BED_LEVELING_BILINEAR on a current Marlin so those edge cases do not apply. Trade-off: babysteps now permanently move your baseline offset, so an accidental large nudge changes the saved value. This is a preference call, hence Medium.","howToApply":"In Configuration_adv.h uncomment #define BABYSTEP_ZPROBE_OFFSET under the BABYSTEPPING block. Reflash, then re-verify offset and M500.","sources":["https://marlinfw.org/docs/gcode/M290.html","https://github.com/MarlinFirmware/Marlin/issues/19277","https://github.com/MarlinFirmware/Marlin/issues/15975"]},{"title":"Calibrate the first layer with an exaggerated test (thick layer, wide line)","priority":"High","currentValue":"n/a (slicer-side test setup)","recommendedValue":"First layer height >= 0.25 mm; first-layer line width >= 120% (e.g. 0.48 mm)","rationale":"Ellis' Print Tuning Guide recommends thicker first layers and wider first-layer lines because they make under/over-squish far more visible and make the saved offset more tolerant of small probe-to-probe variation. Judging squish on a thin 0.12 mm first layer is much harder and less repeatable.","howToApply":"In Orca/Cura: Quality > first layer height 0.25-0.3 mm; Width > first layer line width 120% (or 0.48 mm). Print a single-layer patch test or Ellis' scattered-square pattern.","sources":["https://ellis3dp.com/Print-Tuning-Guide/articles/first_layer_squish.html","https://github.com/AndrewEllis93/Print-Tuning-Guide/blob/main/articles/first_layer_squish.md"]},{"title":"Know the Z-offset sign convention and CRTouch expected range","priority":"Medium","currentValue":"NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET Z = -3.49","recommendedValue":"Keep negative; expect a tuned value roughly in the -2 to -4 mm range. More negative = more squish.","rationale":"For a CRTouch the deployed pin sits ~2-3 mm below the retracted nozzle, so the Z offset is always negative; -3.49 is plausible for this mount. Making M851 Z / NOZZLE_TO_PROBE_OFFSET Z MORE negative moves the nozzle closer to the bed (more squish); less negative lifts it. Getting the sign backwards is a common cause of crashed nozzles or never-touching first layers.","howToApply":"Lower nozzle (more squish): M851 Z-3.60. Raise nozzle (less squish): M851 Z-3.40. Save with M500.","sources":["https://marlinfw.org/docs/gcode/M851.html","https://3dx.info/achieving-perfect-first-layers-installing-and-calibrating-a-bltouch-or-crtouch-sensor-on-your-3d-printer/"]},{"title":"If CRTouch probing is inconsistent, raise the slow probe feedrate or check HS mode","priority":"Low","currentValue":"Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_SLOW 120 mm/min; MULTIPLE_PROBING 2","recommendedValue":"If inconsistent first-layer/mesh: try Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_SLOW up to ~960 mm/min and/or verify high-speed probe behavior; otherwise leave as-is","rationale":"CRTouch units are known to mis-trigger in some high-speed probe modes; community guidance is to raise the Z feedrate to ~960 mm/min or disable HS mode if probing is erratic. Current SLOW=120 mm/min is conservative and generally reliable, and MULTIPLE_PROBING 2 already improves repeatability, so this is only a fallback if you observe inconsistent offsets between prints.","howToApply":"If needed, set #define Z_PROBE_FEEDRATE_SLOW (960) in Configuration.h (or test via M-codes), reflash, re-run G29, and re-verify first layer.","sources":["https://github.com/mriscoc/Ender3V2S1/wiki/3D-BLTouch","https://ellis3dp.com/Print-Tuning-Guide/articles/troubleshooting/first_layer_squish_consistency_issues/z_offset_inconsistency.html"]}]</recommendations>
<imageNeeds>["Side-by-side photos of a printed first-layer test square: good squish vs under-squished (gaps, visible bed) vs over-squished (glassy/wavy/ridged)","Diagram showing CRTouch deployed pin sitting 2-3 mm below the retracted nozzle, illustrating the negative Z offset","Annotated Ender 3 LCD status screen showing the double-click Z babystepping adjustment screen","Ellis-style scattered-square first-layer test pattern laid out across the bed"]</imageNeeds>
